items.title: Oil back above $100 means the watchlist is back under a live inflation-and-yields squeeze
items.summary: Reuters live coverage indicated oil moved back above $100 while Iran's new supreme leader said the Strait of Hormuz should remain closed. Full Reuters live text was not directly accessible in this run, but the source quality is high and the signal is clear: the market is no longer treating the latest reserve-release response as enough to stabilize the energy shock.

items.title: Pentagon carve-out for Anthropic just reduces a live Palantir overhang
items.summary: Reuters reported the Pentagon told senior leaders that Anthropic tools may keep running beyond the previously announced six-month phase-out if they are deemed critical to national security. Full Reuters text was not directly accessible in this run, but the source quality is high and the signal is clear: the Defense Department is leaving itself room to keep important Anthropic-linked workflows alive instead of forcing a hard cutoff.

items.title: Ondas just moved deeper into defense with the BIRD acquisition closing
items.summary: Ondas disclosed in an 8-K that it acquired BIRD Aerosystems and registered 6.93 million shares tied to the deal. The direct signal is strategic, not cosmetic: Ondas is adding airborne missile-protection and ISR products already deployed across more than 700 aircraft and 40-plus aircraft types.

items.title: New U.S. trade probes just reopened tariff risk across the watchlist supply chain
items.summary: Reuters reported the U.S. opened new Section 301 unfair-trade probes that could lead to fresh tariffs by this summer against China, the EU, India, Japan, South Korea, and Mexico. Full Reuters text was not directly accessible in this run, but the source quality is high and the signal is clear: trade risk is broadening again beyond a single-country headline.

items.title: Gulf-shipping attacks just revived the oil/yield squeeze on the watchlist
items.summary: Reuters reported that more ships were struck near the Strait of Hormuz and in Iraqi waters, sending oil higher again and pushing Asian shares lower. That matters because this is not just ongoing war noise; it is fresh evidence that the shipping disruption is widening despite the reserve-release response.

items.title: Tesla reset risk is deepening as Wall Street cuts 2026 delivery expectations again
items.summary: Reuters reported that Tesla investors and analysts are cutting delivery estimates, with some now expecting a third straight year of annual delivery decline and warning that heavy spending on AI/robotaxi/autonomy projects could revive cash-burn pressure. Full Reuters text was not directly accessible in this run, so the read stays tight, but the source quality is high and the signal is clear: the hoped-for 2026 volume rebound is losing credibility.

items.title: Oracle’s AI spending outlook just reinforced the data-center demand case
items.summary: Reuters reported that Oracle said the AI data-center boom should keep revenue above Wall Street expectations through at least 2027, with the stock jumping sharply after earnings. Oracle is not on the watchlist, but the signal matters because it points to sustained enterprise AI infrastructure buildout rather than a near-term spending air pocket.

items.title: Oil is re-accelerating even after the IEA’s record reserve release
items.summary: Reuters reported that oil rebounded as markets doubted whether the IEA’s planned 400 million-barrel strategic-reserve release could offset Iran-war supply risk, and separately that Iran’s military said the world should be prepared for $200 oil. The second point is rhetoric, not a price print, but together they mark a meaningful shift: the market is not treating policy action as enough to fully cap the shock.

items.title: Nvidia commits $2 billion to Nebius in fresh AI-infrastructure bet
items.summary: Reuters reported that Nvidia will invest $2 billion in AI cloud company Nebius, extending its push beyond chips into the infrastructure layer that absorbs GPU demand. Full Reuters article text was not directly accessible in this run, so the read stays tight to the reported investment and stated AI-cloud rationale.

items.title: Ondas buys BIRD and pushes deeper into airborne defense
items.summary: Ondas said it has acquired BIRD Aerosystems, adding airborne missile-protection and ISR systems already deployed across hundreds of aircraft. Full company filing/press-release text was not directly verified in this run, so the read stays narrow to the reported transaction outline.

items.title: IEA oil-release move turns from proposal into action signal
items.summary: Reuters reported the IEA was set to recommend a record strategic-reserve release of about 400 million barrels, and Germany said it would release reserves after the IEA request. That does not end the Iran supply risk, but it raises the odds that policymakers will lean hard against an energy-driven inflation shock.

items.title: Tesla Autopilot probe reportedly takes a more serious legal turn
items.summary: Reuters reported that U.S. prosecutors in the Tesla Autopilot investigation were focusing on securities- and wire-fraud angles. Full article text was not directly accessible in this run, so the interpretation stays tight.

items.title: Oil shock unwinds fast as de-escalation talk hits crude
items.summary: Oil fell sharply and equities rebounded after Trump said the Middle East war could end soon, but the signal is still political rather than a confirmed ceasefire or shipping normalization.

items.title: Middle East war de-escalation rhetoric drove a sharp reversal in crude and a risk-on bounce
items.summary: - Event: Middle East war de-escalation rhetoric drove a sharp reversal in crude and a risk-on bounce.

items.summary: 1. Macro (cross-asset): U.S. announced support measures for Gulf oil shipping risk (insurance; possible naval escort context), while U.S. Treasury yields remained elevated with reduced safe-haven bid in bonds amid Middle East escalation headlines.

items.summary: - Potential material single-name: Broadcom (AVGO) Q1 beat + strong Q2 guide; AI revenue +106% YoY; management line-of-sight to >$100B AI chip revenue by 2027; shares +~5% post-market.
